auth.heading: AI-Medikelizar
auth.subtitle: Clinical Reference Tool
auth.description: Sign in to access evidence-based medical information grounded in trusted sources.

disclaimer: **Not a substitute for professional medical advice.** AI-Medikelizar synthesises information from trusted sources for educational reference only. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider for personal medical decisions.

home.heading: Ask a clinical question
home.subheading: Get answers grounded in peer-reviewed research and trusted health authorities.
home.inputPlaceholder: e.g. What are the standard treatments for adult-onset asthma?
home.searchHint: Press <kbd>Enter</kbd> to search. Higher thoroughness = more sources, longer wait.

results.loadingThorough: Thorough search underway — this may take a moment…
results.adminAccessRequired: Admin access required. Please sign in with an admin account.
results.followupPlaceholder: Ask a follow-up or start a new topic…

sources.heading: Trusted sources
sources.subheading: Every answer from AI-Medikelizar is grounded in evidence from these authoritative databases and health organisations. We index only publicly available, peer-reviewed, or government-vetted content.

pubmed.name: PubMed / MEDLINE
pubmed.publisher: U.S. National Library of Medicine & National Institutes of Health
pubmed.description: Over 35 million citations for biomedical literature from MEDLINE, life science journals, and online books. Citations may include links to full-text content from PubMed Central and publisher web sites.
pubmed.types: Peer-reviewed journal articles, systematic reviews, clinical trials

cdc.name: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC)
cdc.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services
cdc.description: Authoritative public health information, disease-specific guidelines, vaccination recommendations, epidemiological data, and health statistics for the United States and global health threats.
cdc.types: Guidelines, MMWR reports, data sets, clinical resources

who.name: World Health Organization (WHO)
who.description: Global health guidelines, disease classification (ICD), international health regulations, epidemiological updates, and evidence-based recommendations for health policy and clinical practice worldwide.
who.types: Guidelines, technical reports, situation reports, classification tools

medlineplus.name: MedlinePlus
medlineplus.publisher: U.S. National Library of Medicine & National Institutes of Health
medlineplus.description: Consumer-friendly health information covering more than 1,000 health topics in multiple languages. Trusted, up-to-date health information reviewed by medical librarians and physicians.
medlineplus.types: Health topic summaries, drug information, medical encyclopedia, patient handouts

nih.name: National Institutes of Health (NIH)
nih.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services
nih.description: The nation's medical research agency encompassing 27 institutes and centres. Source for clinical trial registries, research funding priorities, disease-specific research summaries, and health disparities data.
nih.types: Research summaries, clinical trial info, health topic portals, strategic plans

pmc.name: PubMed Central (PMC)
pmc.publisher: U.S. National Library of Medicine & National Institutes of Health
pmc.description: Free full-text archive of biomedical and life sciences journal literature. All articles are freely accessible and many include supplementary materials, data sets, and multimedia.
pmc.types: Full-text articles, review papers, clinical trial reports

msd.name: MSD Manuals
msd.publisher: Merck Sharp & Dohme (MSD), a subsidiary of Merck & Co.
msd.description: Trusted, comprehensive medical reference covering thousands of diseases, symptoms, and treatments. Available in both a Professional Edition and a Consumer Edition.
msd.types: Clinical overviews, drug information, diagnostic guidelines, patient education materials, multimedia

sources.noteP1: When you submit a query, AI-Medikelizar searches across the above databases using semantic retrieval to identify the most relevant and authoritative content. Our AI then synthesises a response grounded exclusively in the retrieved evidence — never from its internal training data alone.
sources.noteP2: Every claim in an answer is linked to at least one source citation. You can expand any citation to view the full reference details and access the original publication. We are transparent about the evidence behind every answer.

about.heading: About AI-Medikelizar
about.missionP1: AI-Medikelizar exists to make evidence-based medical knowledge more accessible to clinicians, researchers, and informed patients. We believe that access to reliable, cited health information is a cornerstone of informed decision-making.
about.missionP2: The tool is designed as a clinical reference — not a diagnostic aid, not a treatment planner, and never a substitute for professional judgement. Every answer points back to the source it came from, so you can verify, read in context, and draw your own conclusions.

trustCards.title: Source-restricted retrieval
trustCards.text: Our retrieval system searches only the pre-indexed trusted databases listed on our Sources page. We do not draw from unfiltered web content, forums, or unverified repositories.

trustCards.title: Citation-anchored generation
trustCards.text: The AI model is instructed to compose answers using only the retrieved source text. Every factual statement is tied to a citation marker referencing a specific source card.

trustCards.title: Human-reviewable evidence
trustCards.text: Every source card includes the publication title, authors, journal or publisher, date, and a direct link to the original. You can always read the source for yourself.

trustCards.title: Transparent limitations
trustCards.text: We clearly communicate confidence levels, date ranges of indexed content, and gaps in coverage. No answer is presented as definitive — medicine is an evolving field.

about.techP: AI-Medikelizar uses a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline. When you ask a question, embeddings of your query are used to semantically search a curated corpus of medical literature. The most relevant passages are retrieved and passed to a large language model, which is instructed to:
techItems: Answer only from the provided source text
Mark every claim with an inline citation to its source passage
Explicitly state when the retrieved evidence is insufficient to answer
Never fabricate references or infer beyond what the sources say
intendedItems: Healthcare professionals seeking a quick synthesis of current evidence on a specific clinical question
Researchers exploring a new topic area and looking for key papers
Patients and caregivers who want to better understand a condition or treatment from authoritative sources — in consultation with their healthcare team
about.intendedNote: It is not designed for emergency medical questions, diagnostic decision-making, or as a replacement for clinical judgement.
about.disclaimerP1: AI-Medikelizar is for informational and educational purposes only. The content generated by this tool does not constitute medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always seek the advice of your physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding a medical condition or treatment. Never disregard professional medical advice or delay in seeking it because of something you have read on this site.
about.disclaimerP2: If you think you may have a medical emergency, call your doctor or 911 immediately. AI-Medikelizar does not recommend or endorse any specific tests, physicians, products, procedures, opinions, or other information that may be referenced in generated responses.

pricing.heading: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subheading: Choose the plan that fits your needs. All plans include access to evidence-based medical information from trusted sources. Upgrade anytime.

faq.q: Can I upgrade or downgrade my plan?
faq.a: Yes, you can upgrade or downgrade at any time. Changes take effect immediately. If you downgrade, the new limits apply at the start of your next billing cycle.

faq.q: What happens when I reach my daily query limit?
faq.a: If you reach your daily limit, you can upgrade to a higher tier for more queries, or wait until the next day when your quota resets.
Yes, you can cancel anytime through your account settings. Your access continues until the end of the current billing period.

faq.q: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faq.a: Yes, all paid plans come with a 7-day free trial. You won't be charged until the trial period ends.

footer.disclaimer: This tool does not provide medical advice. See the About page for full disclaimer.
footer.copyright: © {year} AI-Medikelizar. Not a substitute for professional medical advice.

errors.backendTimeout: Backend timed out — falling back to demo
errors.backendNotReachable: Backend not reachable — falling back to demo
errors.backendError: Backend error: {message} — falling back to demo
errors.checkoutConnectionError: Could not connect to payment server. Please try again.
